DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata rich-text status usecase
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api artifact-reference best-practice design-specifications guideline interaction principle purpose scale state variant

DSDS Token and Token Group Definitions

Documentation for individual design tokens and hierarchical token groups. Includes token value representation and platform-specific API mappings. Token groups are flexible organizational units that can represent a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), or a token sub-family (e.g., a single color hue family). Groups can contain tokens, nested groups, or a mix of both, forming an arbitrarily deep hierarchy that mirrors how design systems organize their token architecture.

Source: entities/token.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • tokenValue
  • tokenApi
  • token
  • tokenGroup

tokenValue

A displayable representation of the token's value for documentation tools. This is not the authoritative source of truth for the value — the Design Tokens Format file is. The resolved value provides a human-readable snapshot, the reference traces the alias chain, and the dtcgFile points to the authoritative source.

object

Property Type Required Description
resolved string optional The resolved value as a human-readable string (e.g., '#1a1a1a', '16px', '400', 'rgb(0, 85, 179)').
reference string optional If this token is an alias, the name of the referenced token (e.g., 'color.neutral.900', 'spacing.base').
dtcgFile string optional URI or relative path to the W3C Design Tokens Format (DTCG) file containing the authoritative value.

Example 

[
  {
    "resolved": "#1a1a1a",
    "reference": "color.neutral.900",
    "dtcgFile": "./tokens/color.tokens.json"
  },
  {
    "resolved": "16px",
    "reference": "spacing.base",
    "dtcgFile": "./tokens/spacing.tokens.json"
  },
  {
    "resolved": "#0055b3"
  },
  {
    "resolved": "400",
    "reference": "font.weight.regular"
  },
  {
    "resolved": "0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12)",
    "reference": "shadow.elevation.low",
    "dtcgFile": "./tokens/elevation.tokens.json"
  }
]

tokenApi

Platform-specific identifiers for a token. Keys are platform names (e.g., 'cssCustomProperty', 'scssVariable', 'jsConstant', 'iosToken', 'androidToken', 'designToolVariable') and values are the identifier strings for that platform. Additional platforms can be added without schema changes.

object

Open map: keys are strings, values are string

Example 

[
  {
    "cssCustomProperty": "--color-text-primary",
    "scssVariable": "$color-text-primary",
    "lessVariable": "@color-text-primary",
    "jsConstant": "colorTextPrimary",
    "iosToken": "Color.Text.primary",
    "androidToken": "colorTextPrimary",
    "designToolVariable": "color/text/primary",
    "composeToken": "Color.Text.Primary",
    "flutterConstant": "colorTextPrimary",
    "tailwindClass": "text-primary"
  },
  {
    "cssCustomProperty": "--space-4",
    "scssVariable": "$space-4",
    "jsConstant": "space4"
  },
  {
    "cssCustomProperty": "--font-weight-bold",
    "jsConstant": "fontWeightBold",
    "iosToken": "Font.Weight.bold",
    "androidToken": "fontWeightBold",
    "swiftUIFont": "Font.Weight.bold",
    "composeToken": "Typography.Weight.Bold"
  }
]

token

Documentation for a single design token — its name, type, value, platform API mappings, use cases, guidelines, and accessibility considerations.

object

Property Type Required Description
type "token" required Identifies this entity as a token.
name string required The token name. Token names are intentionally unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions that use dots, slashes, or other separators (e.g., 'color-text-primary', 'color.text.primary', 'color/text/primary').
displayName string required Human-readable name.
description richText required
status statusObject required
tokenType string required The type of token aligned with the DTCG spec (e.g., 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'cubicBezier', 'shadow').
summary string optional A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.
since string optional The version in which this token was introduced.
tags string[] optional Freeform keywords to relate guidelines across categories/disciplines (e.g., 'rtl', 'localization', 'validation').
category string optional The token's semantic category. Standard values: 'base' (raw palette values, not used directly in components), 'semantic' (purpose-driven tokens that map to base values, e.g., 'color-text-primary'), 'component' (tokens scoped to a specific component, e.g., 'button-background'). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case.
value tokenValue optional
aliases string[] optional Names of tokens that alias this token.
api tokenApi optional
guidelines tokenGuideline[] optional All structured documentation for this token, expressed as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Tokens accept general guideline types only (best-practices, use-cases, accessibility, examples, artifact-references). Each guideline has a `type` property that determines its shape. The ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.
links link[] optional External resource links and related artifact references.
$extensions extensions optional

References: richText, statusObject, tokenValue, tokenApi, tokenGuideline, link, extensions

Example 

{
  "type": "token",
  "name": "color-text-primary",
  "displayName": "Text Primary",
  "summary": "Default body text color for light and dark surfaces.",
  "description": "The default color for body text, headings, and labels. Provides the highest-contrast text color for standard reading content on default background surfaces.",
  "status": {
    "status": "stable",
    "platformStatus": {
      "css": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "2.0.0"
      },
      "ios": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "2.1.0"
      },
      "android": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "2.2.0"
      },
      "figma": {
        "status": "stable",
        "since": "2.0.0"
      }
    }
  },
  "since": "2.0.0",
  "tags": [
    "color",
    "text",
    "body"
  ],
  "tokenType": "color",
  "category": "semantic",
  "value": {
    "resolved": "#1a1a1a",
    "reference": "color.neutral.900",
    "dtcgFile": "./tokens/color.tokens.json"
  },
  "aliases": [
    "color.text.default",
    "color.text.body"
  ],
  "api": {
    "cssCustomProperty": "--color-text-primary",
    "scssVariable": "$color-text-primary",
    "jsConstant": "colorTextPrimary",
    "iosToken": "Color.Text.primary",
    "androidToken": "colorTextPrimary",
    "designToolVariable": "color/text/primary"
  },
  "guidelines": [
    {
      "type": "purpose",
      "whenToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When applying color to body text, headings, and form labels on default background surfaces."
        },
        {
          "description": "When building components that display primary reading content that must adapt across color modes."
        }
      ],
      "whenNotToUse": [
        {
          "description": "When placing text on dark or colored background surfaces.",
          "alternative": {
            "name": "color-text-inverse",
            "rationale": "This token is optimized for contrast against light backgrounds. Using it on dark or saturated surfaces will fail contrast requirements."
          }
        },
        {
          "description": "When the text is inside a component that supplies its own scoped color tokens (e.g., text on a filled button).",
          "alternative": {
            "name": "color-text-on-action",
            "rationale": "Component-scoped tokens account for the specific background they sit on. Applying a general text token to a scoped context risks contrast failures."
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "best-practices",
      "items": [
        {
          "guidance": "Use for all body text, headings, and form labels on default background surfaces.",
          "rationale": "A single primary text color ensures visual consistency and meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements against the system's default background.",
          "entryType": "encouraged",
          "category": "visual-design"
        },
        {
          "guidance": "Do not override this token's value at the component level. Use color-text-secondary or color-text-tertiary for reduced emphasis.",
          "rationale": "Overriding the primary text color creates inconsistency. The system provides lower-emphasis text tokens for visual hierarchy.",
          "entryType": "prohibited",
          "category": "visual-design"
        },
        {
          "guidance": "This color meets a 15.3:1 contrast ratio against color-background-default in light mode.",
          "rationale": "Exceeds WCAG 2.1 AAA requirements (7:1 for normal text), ensuring readability for users with low vision.",
          "entryType": "informational",
          "category": "accessibility",
          "criteria": [
            "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-minimum",
            "https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#contrast-enhanced"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "type": "accessibility",
      "wcagLevel": "AAA",
      "colorContrast": [
        {
          "foreground": "color-text-primary",
          "background": "color-background-default",
          "contrastRatio": 15.3,
          "level": "AAA",
          "context": "Primary text on default background in light mode."
        },
        {
          "foreground": "color-text-primary",
          "background": "color-background-subtle",
          "contrastRatio": 13.8,
          "level": "AAA",
          "context": "Primary text on subtle background (card surface) in light mode."
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "links": [
    {
      "type": "source",
      "url": "https://code.acme.com/design-system/src/tokens/color/text.tokens.json",
      "label": "Token source file"
    },
    {
      "type": "documentation",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-primary",
      "label": "Token documentation"
    },
    {
      "type": "related",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-secondary",
      "label": "color-text-secondary"
    },
    {
      "type": "related",
      "url": "https://design.acme.com/tokens/color-text-tertiary",
      "label": "color-text-tertiary"
    }
  ]
}

tokenGroup

Documentation for a group of related design tokens. Token groups are flexible organizational units that can represent a full token collection, a token family (e.g., all color tokens), a token sub-family (e.g., a single color hue with its grade scale), or any other logical grouping. Groups can be nested to form an arbitrarily deep hierarchy — a top-level group might represent 'color', containing child groups for 'color-text', 'color-background', and 'color-border', each of which contains individual tokens. This recursive structure mirrors the way real design systems organize tokens into families and sub-families.

object

Property Type Required Description
type "token-group" required Identifies this entity as a token-group.
name string required The token group name. Token group names are intentionally unconstrained by a pattern to accommodate DTCG and design tool naming conventions (e.g., 'color-text', 'color.text', 'color/text').
displayName string required Human-readable name (e.g., 'Text Colors', 'Spacing Scale', 'Red — Pushpin').
description richText required A description of the group's purpose, the tokens it contains, and how they relate to each other. CommonMark supported.
status statusObject required
aliases string[] optional Alternative names this token group is known by across teams, tools, or legacy systems. Useful for search, migration mapping, and cross-referencing.
summary string optional A one-line, plain-text summary. MUST NOT contain markup.
since string optional The version in which this group was introduced.
tags string[] optional Freeform keywords for categorization and search (e.g., 'color', 'palette', 'semantic').
tokenType string optional When all tokens in this group share the same DTCG token type, it MAY be declared here to avoid repetition on each child token. Individual token objects within children MAY override this value. Common values: 'color', 'dimension', 'fontFamily', 'fontWeight', 'duration', 'number', 'shadow'.
category string optional When all tokens in this group share the same semantic category, it MAY be declared here. Standard values: 'base', 'semantic', 'component'. Individual token objects within children MAY override this value.
children token | tokenGroup[] optional An ordered array of tokens and/or nested token groups that belong to this group. Each item MUST be either a token (identified by the presence of `tokenType` and absence of `children`) or a tokenGroup (identified by the presence of `children` or the absence of `tokenType`). The ordering is significant — tools SHOULD preserve the order for display, as it often represents a meaningful progression (e.g., a color grade scale from lightest to darkest, or a spacing scale from smallest to largest).
Min items: 1
guidelines tokenGuideline[] optional All structured documentation for this token group, expressed as an ordered array of typed guideline objects. Token groups accept general guideline types only (best-practices, use-cases, accessibility, examples, artifact-references). Guidelines that apply to the group as a whole live here. Guidelines specific to individual tokens SHOULD be placed on the token within children.
links link[] optional External resource links related to this token group.
$extensions extensions optional

References: richText, statusObject, token, tokenGroup, tokenGuideline, link, extensions

↑ Back to top

Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) 0.1 — Draft Specification
